v.CNV: 耶稣又用比喻对他们说：“有一个人栽种了一个葡萄园，四面围上篱笆，挖了一个压酒池，盖了一座瞭望台，然后租给佃户，就远行去了。
v.ESV: And he began to speak to them in parables. “A man planted a vineyard and put a fence around it and dug a pit for the winepress and built a tower, and leased it to tenants and went into another country.
v.GAE: 예수께서 비유로 그들에게 말씀하시되 한 사람이 포도원을 만들어 산울타리로 두르고 즙 짜는 틀을 만들고 망대를 지어서 농부들에게 세로 주고 타국에 갔더니
v.KJV: And he began to speak unto them by parables. A certain man planted a vineyard, and set an hedge about it, and digged a place for the winefat, and built a tower, and let it out to husbandmen, and went into a far country.
v.NASB: And He began to speak to them in parables: 'A man PLANTED A VINEYARD AND PUT A WALL AROUND IT, AND DUG A VAT UNDER THE WINE PRESS AND BUILT A TOWER, and rented it out to vine-growers and went on a journey.
v.NIV: Jesus then began to speak to them in parables: “A man planted a vineyard. He put a wall around it, dug a pit for the winepress and built a watchtower. Then he rented the vineyard to some farmers and moved to another place.
v.NRSV: Then he began to speak to them in parables. “A man planted a vineyard, put a fence around it, dug a pit for the wine press, and built a watchtower; then he leased it to tenants and went to another country.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 비유로 말씀하기 시작하셨다. "어떤 사람이 포도원을 일구어서, 울타리를 치고, 포도즙을 짜는 확을 파고, 망대를 세웠다. 그리고 그것을 농부들에게 세로 주고, 멀리 떠났다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 그들에게 비유를 들어 말씀하기 시작하셨습니다. “어떤 사람이 포도원을 하나 만들어 울타리를 치고 땅을 파서 포도즙 짜는 틀 자리를 만들고 망대를 세웠다. 그러고는 어떤 농부들에게 포도원을 세주고 멀리 떠났다.

v.CNV: 到了时候，园主派了一个仆人到佃户那里，向佃户收取葡萄园一部分的果子。
v.ESV: When the season came, he sent a servant to the tenants to get from them some of the fruit of the vineyard.
v.GAE: 때가 이르매 농부들에게 포도원 소출 얼마를 받으려고 한 종을 보내니
v.KJV: And at the season he sent to the husbandmen a servant, that he might receive from the husbandmen of the fruit of the vineyard.
v.NASB: 'At the harvest time he sent a slave to the vine-growers, in order to receive some of the produce of the vineyard from the vine-growers.
v.NIV: At harvest time he sent a servant to the tenants to collect from them some of the fruit of the vineyard.
v.NRSV: When the season came, he sent a slave to the tenants to collect from them his share of the produce of the vineyard.
v.SAENEW: 때가 되어서, 주인은 농부들에게서 포도원 소출의 얼마를 받으려고 한 종을 농부들에게 보냈다.
v.WLB: 수확할 때가 되자 주인은 포도원에서 난 소출 가운데 얼마를 받아 오라고 종을 농부들에게 보냈다.

v.CNV: 佃户抓住他，打了他，放他空手回去。
v.ESV: And they took him and beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
v.GAE: 그들이 종을 잡아 심히 때리고 거저 보내었거늘
v.KJV: And they caught him, and beat him, and sent him away empty.
v.NASB: 'They took him, and beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
v.NIV: But they seized him, beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
v.NRSV: But they seized him, and beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.
v.SAENEW: 그런데 그들은 그 종을 잡아서 때리고, 빈 손으로 돌려보냈다.
v.WLB: 그런데 그들은 그 종을 잡아다가 때리고는 빈손으로 돌려보냈다.

v.CNV: 园主再派另外一个仆人到他们那里，他们打伤了他的头，并且侮辱他。
v.ESV: Again he sent to them another servant, and they struck him on the head and treated him shamefully.
v.GAE: 다시 다른 종을 보내니 그의 머리에 상처를 내고 능욕하였거늘
v.KJV: And again he sent unto them another servant; and at him they cast stones, and wounded him in the head, and sent him away shamefully handled.
v.NASB: 'Again he sent them another slave, and they wounded him in the head, and treated him shamefully.
v.NIV: Then he sent another servant to them; they struck this man on the head and treated him shamefully.
v.NRSV: And again he sent another slave to them; this one they beat over the head and insulted.
v.SAENEW: 주인이 다시 다른 종을 농부들에게 보냈다. 그랬더니 그들은 그 종의 머리를 때리고, 그를 능욕하였다.
v.WLB: 그러자 주인은 그들에게 다른 종을 보냈다. 그러나 그들은 그 종의 머리를 때리고 모욕했다.

v.CNV: 园主又派另一个去，他们就把他杀了。后来又派去许多仆人，有的给他们打了，有的给他们杀了。
v.ESV: And he sent another, and him they killed. And so with many others: some they beat, and some they killed.
v.GAE: 또 다른 종을 보내니 그들이 그를 죽이고 또 그 외 많은 종들도 더러는 때리고 더러는 죽인지라
v.KJV: And again he sent another; and him they killed, and many others; beating some, and killing some.
v.NASB: 'And he sent another, and that one they killed; and so with many others, beating some and killing others.
v.NIV: He sent still another, and that one they killed. He sent many others; some of them they beat, others they killed.
v.NRSV: Then he sent another, and that one they killed. And so it was with many others; some they beat, and others they killed.
v.SAENEW: 주인이 또 다른 종을 농부들에게 보냈더니, 그들은 그 종을 죽였다. 그래서 또 다른 종을 많이 보냈는데, 더러는 때리고, 더러는 죽였다.
v.WLB: 주인은 또 다른 종을 보냈지만 그들은 그 종을 죽여 버렸다. 그러고 나서도 주인은 계속해서 다른 종들을 많이 보냈는데 농부들은 그 종들을 때리고 더러는 죽이기도 했다.

v.CNV: 还有一个，就是园主的爱子，最后园主派他到那里去，说：‘他们必尊敬我的儿子。’
v.ESV: He had still one other, a beloved son. Finally he sent him to them, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
v.GAE: 이제 한 사람이 남았으니 곧 그가 사랑하는 아들이라 최후로 이를 보내며 이르되 내 아들은 존대하리라 하였더니
v.KJV: Having yet therefore one son, his wellbeloved, he sent him also last unto them, saying, They will reverence my son.
v.NASB: 'He had one more to send, a beloved son; he sent him last of all to them, saying, 'They will respect my son.'
v.NIV: “He had one left to send, a son, whom he loved. He sent him last of all, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
v.NRSV: He had still one other, a beloved son. Finally he sent him to them, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
v.SAENEW: 이제 그 주인에게는 단 한 사람, 곧 사랑하는 아들이 남아 있었다. 마지막으로 그 아들을 그들에게 보내며 말하기를 '그들이 내 아들이야 존중하겠지' 하였다.
v.WLB: 주인에게는 이제 단 한 사람, 바로 사랑하는 자기 아들이 남아 있었다. 그는 마지막으로 아들을 보내면서 ‘그들이 내 아들은 존중하겠지’라고 말했다.

v.CNV: 那些佃户却彼此说：‘这是继承产业的；来，我们杀了他，产业就是我们的了。’
v.ESV: But those tenants said to one another, ‘This is the heir. Come, let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’
v.GAE: 그 농부들이 서로 말하되 이는 상속자니 자 죽이자 그러면 그 유산이 우리 것이 되리라 하고
v.KJV: But those husbandmen said among themselves, This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and the inheritance shall be ours.
v.NASB: 'But those vine-growers said to one another, 'This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours!'
v.NIV: “But the tenants said to one another, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’
v.NRSV: But those tenants said to one another, ‘This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’
v.SAENEW: 그러나 그 농부들은 서로 말하였다. '이 사람은 상속자다. 그를 죽여 버리자. 그러면 유산은 우리의 차지가 될 것이다.'
v.WLB: 그러나 농부들은 자기들끼리 수군거렸다. ‘이 사람은 상속자다. 가서 그를 죽이자. 그러면 그 유산은 우리 차지가 될 것이다.’

v.CNV: 于是抓住他，把他杀了，扔在葡萄园外。
v.ESV: And they took him and killed him and threw him out of the vineyard.
v.GAE: 이에 잡아 죽여 포도원 밖에 내던졌느니라
v.KJV: And they took him, and killed him, and cast him out of the vineyard.
v.NASB: 'They took him, and killed him and threw him out of the vineyard.
v.NIV: So they took him and killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
v.NRSV: So they seized him, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
v.SAENEW: 그러면서, 그들은 그를 잡아서 죽이고, 포도원 바깥에다가 내던졌다.
v.WLB: 그리하여 농부들은 아들을 데려다가 죽이고 포도원 밖으로 내던져 버렸다.

v.CNV: 这样，葡萄园的主人要怎么办呢？他要来除掉那些佃户，把葡萄园租给别人。
v.ESV: What will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the tenants and give the vineyard to others.
v.GAE: 포도원 주인이 어떻게 하겠느냐 와서 그 농부들을 진멸하고 포도원을 다른 사람들에게 주리라
v.KJV: What shall therefore the lord of the vineyard do? he will come and destroy the husbandmen, and will give the vineyard unto others.
v.NASB: 'What will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the vine-growers, and will give the vineyard to others.
v.NIV: “What then will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and kill those tenants and give the vineyard to others.
v.NRSV: What then will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the tenants and give the vineyard to others.
v.SAENEW: 그러니, 포도원 주인이 어떻게 하겠느냐? 그는 와서 농부들을 죽이고, 포도원을 다른 사람들에게 줄 것이다.
v.WLB: 이렇게 되면 포도원 주인이 어떻게 하겠느냐? 그가 와서 그 농부들을 죽이고 다른 사람들에게 포도원을 줄 것이다.

v.CNV: 你们没有念过这段圣经吗：‘建筑工人所弃的石头，成了房角的主要石头；
v.ESV: Have you not read this Scripture: “‘The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone;
v.GAE: 너희가 성경에 건축자들이 버린 돌이 모퉁이의 머릿돌이 되었나니
v.KJV: And have ye not read this scripture; The stone which the builders rejected is become the head of the corner:
v.NASB: 'Have you not even read this Scripture: 'THE STONE WHICH THE BUILDERS REJECTED, THIS BECAME THE CHIEF CORNER stone;
v.NIV: Haven’t you read this passage of Scripture: “‘The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone;
v.NRSV: Have you not read this scripture: ‘The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone;
v.SAENEW: 너희는 성경에서 이런 말씀도 읽어 보지 못하였느냐? '집을 짓는 사람이 버린 돌이 집 모퉁이의 머릿돌이 되었다.
v.WLB: 너희는 성경에서 이런 말씀을 읽어 보지 못했느냐? ‘건축자들이 버린 돌이 집 모퉁이의 머릿돌이 됐다.

v.CNV: 这是主所作的，在我们眼中看为希奇。’”
v.ESV: this was the Lord's doing, and it is marvelous in our eyes’?”
v.GAE: 이것은 주로 말미암아 된 것이요 우리 눈에 놀랍도다 함을 읽어 보지도 못하였느냐 하시니라
v.KJV: This was the Lord’s doing, and it is marvellous in our eyes?
v.NASB: THIS CAME ABOUT FROM THE LORD, AND IT IS MARVELOUS IN OUR EYES '?'
v.NIV: the Lord has done this, and it is marvelous in our eyes’ ?”
v.NRSV: this was the Lord’s doing, and it is amazing in our eyes’?”
v.SAENEW: 이것은 주님께서 하신 일이요, 우리 눈에는 놀랍게 보인다.'"
v.WLB: 주께서 이렇게 하셨으니 우리 눈에 놀랍게 보일 뿐이다.’”

v.CNV: 他们知道他这比喻是针对他们说的，就想要捉拿他，但因为害怕群众，只好离开他走了。
v.ESV: And they were seeking to arrest him but feared the people, for they perceived that he had told the parable against them. So they left him and went away.
v.GAE: 그들이 예수의 이 비유가 자기들을 가리켜 말씀하심인 줄 알고 잡고자 하되 무리를 두려워하여 예수를 두고 가니라
v.KJV: And they sought to lay hold on him, but feared the people: for they knew that he had spoken the parable against them: and they left him, and went their way.
v.NASB: And they were seeking to seize Him, and yet they feared the people, for they understood that He spoke the parable against them. And so they left Him and went away.
v.NIV: Then the chief priests, the teachers of the law and the elders looked for a way to arrest him because they knew he had spoken the parable against them. But they were afraid of the crowd; so they left him and went away.
v.NRSV: When they realized that he had told this parable against them, they wanted to arrest him, but they feared the crowd. So they left him and went away.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 이 비유가 자기들을 겨냥하여 하신 말씀인 줄 알아차리고, 예수를 잡으려고 하였다. 그러나 그들은 무리를 두려워하여, 예수를 그대로 두고 떠나갔다.
v.WLB: 그러자 그들은 예수께서 말씀하신 이 비유가 자기들을 가리켜 말씀하시는 것임을 알아차리고 예수를 체포할 방도를 모색했습니다. 그러나 백성들을 두려워해 예수를 그대로 두고 가 버렸습니다.

v.CNV: 后来，他们派了几个法利赛人和希律党的人到耶稣那里去，要找他的把柄来陷害他。
v.ESV: And they sent to him some of the Pharisees and some of the Herodians, to trap him in his talk.
v.GAE: 그들이 예수의 말씀을 책잡으려 하여 바리새인과 헤롯당 중에서 사람을 보내매
v.KJV: And they send unto him certain of the Pharisees and of the Herodians, to catch him in his words.
v.NASB: Then they sent some of the Pharisees and Herodians to Him in order to trap Him in a statement.
v.NIV: Later they sent some of the Pharisees and Herodians to Jesus to catch him in his words.
v.NRSV: Then they sent to him some Pharisees and some Herodians to trap him in what he said.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 말로 예수를 책잡으려고, 바리새파 사람들과 헤롯 당원 가운데서 몇 사람을 예수께로 보냈다.
v.WLB: 그들은 예수의 말씀을 트집 잡아 보려고 바리새파 사람들과 헤롯 당원들을 예수께로 보냈습니다.

v.CNV: 他们来到了，就对他说：“老师，我们知道你为人诚实，不顾忌任何人，因为你不徇情面，只照着真理把　神的道教导人。请问纳税给凯撒可以不可以？我们该纳不该纳呢？”
v.ESV: And they came and said to him, “Teacher, we know that you are true and do not care about anyone's opinion. For you are not swayed by appearances, but truly teach the way of God. Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar, or not? Should we pay them, or should we not?”
v.GAE: 와서 이르되 선생님이여 우리가 아노니 당신은 참되시고 아무도 꺼리는 일이 없으시니 이는 사람을 외모로 보지 않고 오직 진리로써 하나님의 도를 가르치심이니이다 가이사에게 세금을 바치는 것이 옳으니이까 옳지 아니하니이까
v.KJV: And when they were come, they say unto him, Master, we know that thou art true, and carest for no man: for thou regardest not the person of men, but teachest the way of God in truth: Is it lawful to give tribute to Caesar, or not?
v.NASB: They came and said to Him, 'Teacher, we know that You are truthful and defer to no one; for You are not partial to any, but teach the way of God in truth. Is it lawful to pay a poll-tax to Caesar, or not?
v.NIV: They came to him and said, “Teacher, we know that you are a man of integrity. You aren’t swayed by others, because you pay no attention to who they are; but you teach the way of God in accordance with the truth. Is it right to pay the imperial tax to Caesar or not?
v.NRSV: And they came and said to him, “Teacher, we know that you are sincere, and show deference to no one; for you do not regard people with partiality, but teach the way of God in accordance with truth. Is it lawful to pay taxes to the emperor, or not?
v.SAENEW: 그들이 와서, 예수께 말하였다. "선생님, 우리는, 선생님이 진실한 분이시고 아무에게도 매이지 않는 분이심을 압니다. 선생님은 사람의 겉모습으로 판단하지 않으시고, 하나님의 길을 참되게 가르치십니다. 그런데, 황제에게 세금을 바치는 것이 옳습니까, 옳지 않습니까? 바쳐야 합니까, 바치지 말아야 합니까?"
v.WLB: 그들은 예수께 다가와 말했습니다. “선생님, 우리는 선생님을 참된 분으로 알고 있습니다. 선생님은 사람을 겉모습으로 판단하지 않기 때문에 사람으로 인해 동요되지 않고 하나님의 진리를 참되게 가르치신다고 들었습니다. 그런데 우리가 가이사에게 세금을 내는 것이 옳습니까, 옳지 않습니까?

v.CNV: 耶稣看出他们的假意，就对他们说：“你们为什么试探我呢？拿一个银币来给我，让我看看。”
v.ESV: But, knowing their hypocrisy, he said to them, “Why put me to the test? Bring me a denarius and let me look at it.”
v.GAE: 우리가 바치리이까 말리이까 한대 예수께서 그 외식함을 아시고 이르시되 어찌하여 나를 시험하느냐 데나리온 하나를 가져다가 내게 보이라 하시니
v.KJV: Shall we give, or shall we not give? But he, knowing their hypocrisy, said unto them, Why tempt ye me? bring me a penny, that I may see it.
v.NASB: 'Shall we pay or shall we not pay?' But He, knowing their hypocrisy, said to them, 'Why are you testing Me? Bring Me a denarius to look at.'
v.NIV: Should we pay or shouldn’t we?” But Jesus knew their hypocrisy. “Why are you trying to trap me?” he asked. “Bring me a denarius and let me look at it.”
v.NRSV: Should we pay them, or should we not?” But knowing their hypocrisy, he said to them, “Why are you putting me to the test? Bring me a denarius and let me see it.”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들의 속임수를 아시고, 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "어찌하여 나를 시험하느냐? 데나리온 한 닢을 가져다가, 나에게 보여보아라."
v.WLB: 우리가 세금을 내야 합니까, 내지 말아야 합니까?” 예수께서는 위선적인 그들의 속셈을 다 아시고 말씀하셨습니다. “왜 너희가 나를 시험하느냐? 데나리온 동전 하나를 가져와 내게 보이라.”

v.CNV: 他们就拿来了。耶稣问他们：“这是谁的像，谁的名号？”他们回答他：“凯撒的。”
v.ESV: And they brought one. And he said to them, “Whose likeness and inscription is this?” They said to him, “Caesar's.”
v.GAE: 가져왔거늘 예수께서 이르시되 이 형상과 이 글이 누구의 것이냐 이르되 가이사의 것이니이다
v.KJV: And they brought it. And he saith unto them, Whose is this image and superscription? And they said unto him, Caesar’s.
v.NASB: They brought one. And He said to them, 'Whose likeness and inscription is this?' And they said to Him, 'Caesar's.'
v.NIV: They brought the coin, and he asked them, “Whose image is this? And whose inscription?” “Caesar’s,” they replied.
v.NRSV: And they brought one. Then he said to them, “Whose head is this, and whose title?” They answered, “The emperor’s.”
v.SAENEW: 그들이 그것을 가져오니, 예수께서 그들에게 물으셨다. "이 초상은 누구의 것이며, 적힌 글자는 누구의 것이냐?" 그들이 대답하였다. "황제의 것입니다."
v.WLB: 그들이 동전 하나를 가져오자 예수께서 그들에게 물으셨습니다. “동전에 있는 얼굴과 새겨진 글자가 누구의 것이냐?” 그들은 “가이사의 것입니다” 하고 대답했습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣说：“凯撒的应当归给凯撒，　神的应当归给　神。”他们就对他十分惊奇。
v.ESV: Jesus said to them, “Render to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's.” And they marveled at him.
v.GAE: 이에 예수께서 이르시되 가이사의 것은 가이사에게, 하나님의 것은 하나님께 바치라 하시니 그들이 예수께 대하여 매우 놀랍게 여기더라
v.KJV: And Jesus answering said unto them, Render to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s. And they marvelled at him.
v.NASB: And Jesus said to them, 'Render to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's.' And they were amazed at Him.
v.NIV: Then Jesus said to them, “Give back to Caesar what is Caesar’s and to God what is God’s.” And they were amazed at him.
v.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Give to the emperor the things that are the emperor’s, and to God the things that are God’s.” And they were utterly amazed at him.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "황제의 것은 황제에게 돌려주고, 하나님의 것은 하나님께 돌려드려라." 그들은 예수께 경탄하였다.
v.WLB: 그러자 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨습니다. “가이사의 것은 가이사에게 바치고 하나님의 것은 하나님께 바치라.” 그들은 예수께 몹시 감탄했습니다.

v.CNV: 撒都该人向来认为没有复活的事，他们来到耶稣那里，问他：
v.ESV: And Sadducees came to him, who say that there is no resurrection. And they asked him a question, saying,
v.GAE: 부활이 없다 하는 사두개인들이 예수께 와서 물어 이르되
v.KJV: Then come unto him the Sadducees, which say there is no resurrection; and they asked him, saying,
v.NASB: Some Sadducees (who say that there is no resurrection) came to Jesus, and began questioning Him, saying,
v.NIV: Then the Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to him with a question.
v.NRSV: Some Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to him and asked him a question, saying,
v.SAENEW: 부활이 없다고 주장하는 사두개파 사람들이 예수께 와서, 물었다.
v.WLB: 부활이 없다고 주장하는 사두개파 사람들이 예수께 와서 물었습니다.

v.CNV: “老师，摩西曾写给我们说：‘如果一个人死了，留下妻子，没有儿女，他的弟弟就应当娶他的妻子，为哥哥立后。’
v.ESV: “Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man's brother dies and leaves a wife, but leaves no child, the man must take the widow and raise up offspring for his brother.
v.GAE: 선생님이여 모세가 우리에게 써 주기를 어떤 사람의 형이 자식이 없이 아내를 두고 죽으면 그 동생이 그 아내를 취하여 형을 위하여 상속자를 세울지니라 하였나이다
v.KJV: Master, Moses wrote unto us, If a man’s brother die, and leave his wife behind him, and leave no children, that his brother should take his wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.
v.NASB: 'Teacher, Moses wrote for us that IF A MAN'S BROTHER DIES and leaves behind a wife AND LEAVES NO CHILD, HIS BROTHER SHOULD MARRY THE WIFE AND RAISE UP CHILDREN TO HIS BROTHER.
v.NIV: “Teacher,” they said, “Moses wrote for us that if a man’s brother dies and leaves a wife but no children, the man must marry the widow and raise up offspring for his brother.
v.NRSV: “Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man’s brother dies, leaving a wife but no child, the man shall marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.
v.SAENEW: "선생님, 모세가 우리에게 써 주기를 '어떤 사람의 형이 자식이 없이, 아내만 남겨 두고 죽으면, 그 동생이 그 형수를 맞아들여서, 그의 형에게 대를 이을 자식을 낳아 주어야 한다' 하였습니다.
v.WLB: “선생님, 모세가 우리를 위해 쓰기를 ‘만약 형이 자식 없이 아내만 남겨 놓고 죽으면 동생이 그 형수와 결혼해서 형의 대를 이을 자식을 낳아야 한다’고 했습니다.

v.CNV: 从前有兄弟七人，头一个娶了妻子，死了，没有留下孩子；
v.ESV: There were seven brothers; the first took a wife, and when he died left no offspring.
v.GAE: 칠 형제가 있었는데 맏이가 아내를 취하였다가 상속자가 없이 죽고
v.KJV: Now there were seven brethren: and the first took a wife, and dying left no seed.
v.NASB: 'There were seven brothers; and the first took a wife, and died leaving no children.
v.NIV: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married and died without leaving any children.
v.NRSV: There were seven brothers; the first married and, when he died, left no children;
v.SAENEW: 형제가 일곱 있었습니다. 그런데, 맏이가 아내를 얻었는데, 죽을 때에 자식을 남기지 못하였습니다.
v.WLB: 그런데 일곱 형제들이 있었습니다. 첫째가 결혼을 했는데 자식 없이 죽었습니다.

v.CNV: 第二个娶了她，也没有留下孩子，就死了；第三个也是这样。
v.ESV: And the second took her, and died, leaving no offspring. And the third likewise.
v.GAE: 둘째도 그 여자를 취하였다가 상속자가 없이 죽고 셋째도 그렇게 하여
v.KJV: And the second took her, and died, neither left he any seed: and the third likewise.
v.NASB: 'The second one married her, and died leaving behind no children; and the third likewise;
v.NIV: The second one married the widow, but he also died, leaving no child. It was the same with the third.
v.NRSV: and the second married the widow and died, leaving no children; and the third likewise;
v.SAENEW: 그리하여 둘째가 그 형수를 맞아들였는데, 그도 또한 자식을 남기지 못하고 죽고, 셋째도 그러하였습니다.
v.WLB: 둘째가 그 형수와 결혼을 했는데 그도 역시 자식 없이 죽었습니다. 셋째도 마찬가지였습니다.

v.CNV: 那七个人都没有留下孩子，最后那女人也死了。
v.ESV: And the seven left no offspring. Last of all the woman also died.
v.GAE: 일곱이 다 상속자가 없었고 최후에 여자도 죽었나이다
v.KJV: And the seven had her, and left no seed: last of all the woman died also.
v.NASB: and so all seven left no children. Last of all the woman died also.
v.NIV: In fact, none of the seven left any children. Last of all, the woman died too.
v.NRSV: none of the seven left children. Last of all the woman herself died.
v.SAENEW: 일곱이 모두 자식을 두지 못하였습니다. 맨 마지막으로 그 여자도 죽었습니다.
v.WLB: 그렇게 일곱 형제 모두가 자식 없이 죽었고 결국 그 여자도 죽었습니다.

v.CNV: 到了复活的时候，他们都要复活，她是哪一个的妻子呢？因为七个人都娶过她。”
v.ESV: In the resurrection, when they rise again, whose wife will she be? For the seven had her as wife.”
v.GAE: 일곱 사람이 다 그를 아내로 취하였으니 부활 때 곧 그들이 살아날 때에 그 중의 누구의 아내가 되리이까
v.KJV: In the resurrection therefore, when they shall rise, whose wife shall she be of them? for the seven had her to wife.
v.NASB: 'In the resurrection, when they rise again, which one's wife will she be? For all seven had married her.'
v.NIV: At the resurrection whose wife will she be, since the seven were married to her?”
v.NRSV: In the resurrection whose wife will she be? For the seven had married her.”
v.SAENEW: [그들이 살아날] 부활 때에, 그 여자는 그들 가운데 누구의 아내가 되겠습니까? 일곱이 모두 그 여자를 아내로 맞아들였으니 말입니다."
v.WLB: 일곱 형제들이 다 이 여자와 결혼을 했으니 부활할 때에 이 여자는 누구의 아내가 되겠습니까?”

v.CNV: 耶稣对他们说：“你们错了，不正是因为你们不明白圣经，也不晓得　神的能力吗？
v.ESV: Jesus said to them, “Is this not the reason you are wrong, because you know neither the Scriptures nor the power of God?
v.GAE: 예수께서 이르시되 너희가 성경도 하나님의 능력도 알지 못하므로 오해함이 아니냐
v.KJV: And Jesus answering said unto them, Do ye not therefore err, because ye know not the scriptures, neither the power of God?
v.NASB: Jesus said to them, 'Is this not the reason you are mistaken, that you do not understand the Scriptures or the power of God?
v.NIV: Jesus replied, “Are you not in error because you do not know the Scriptures or the power of God?
v.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Is not this the reason you are wrong, that you know neither the scriptures nor the power of God?
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "너희는 성경도 모르고, 하나님의 능력도 모르니까, 잘못 생각하는 것이 아니냐?
v.WLB: 예수께서 대답하셨습니다. “너희가 성경도 모르고 하나님의 능력도 몰라서 그렇게 잘못 생각하는 것이 아니냐?

v.CNV: 因为人从死里复活以后，也不娶，也不嫁，而是像天上的天使一样。
v.ESV: For when they rise from the dead, they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.
v.GAE: 사람이 죽은 자 가운데서 살아날 때에는 장가도 아니 가고 시집도 아니 가고 하늘에 있는 천사들과 같으니라
v.KJV: For when they shall rise from the dead, they neither marry, nor are given in marriage; but are as the angels which are in heaven.
v.NASB: 'For when they rise from the dead, they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.
v.NIV: When the dead rise, they will neither marry nor be given in marriage; they will be like the angels in heaven.
v.NRSV: For when they rise from the dead, they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.
v.SAENEW: 사람이 죽은 사람들 가운데서 살아날 때에는, 장가도 가지 않고 시집도 가지 않고, 하늘에 있는 천사들과 같다.
v.WLB: 죽은 사람들이 살아날 때에는 시집도 가지 않고 장가도 가는 일이 없다. 그들은 마치 하늘에 있는 천사들같이 될 것이다.

v.CNV: 关于死人复活的事，摩西的经卷中荆棘篇上，　神怎样对他说：‘我是亚伯拉罕的　神，以撒的　神，雅各的　神’，你们没有念过吗？
v.ESV: And as for the dead being raised, have you not read in the book of Moses, in the passage about the bush, how God spoke to him, saying, ‘I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’?
v.GAE: 죽은 자가 살아난다는 것을 말할진대 너희가 모세의 책 중 가시나무 떨기에 관한 글에 하나님께서 모세에게 이르시되 나는 아브라함의 하나님이요 이삭의 하나님이요 야곱의 하나님이로라 하신 말씀을 읽어보지 못하였느냐
v.KJV: And as touching the dead, that they rise: have ye not read in the book of Moses, how in the bush God spake unto him, saying, I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?
v.NASB: 'But regarding the fact that the dead rise again, have you not read in the book of Moses, in the passage about the burning bush, how God spoke to him, saying, 'I AM THE GOD OF ABRAHAM, AND THE GOD OF ISAAC, and the God of Jacob '?
v.NIV: Now about the dead rising—have you not read in the Book of Moses, in the account of the burning bush, how God said to him, ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’ ?
v.NRSV: And as for the dead being raised, have you not read in the book of Moses, in the story about the bush, how God said to him, ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’?
v.SAENEW: 죽은 사람들이 살아나는 일에 관해서는, 모세의 책에 떨기나무 이야기가 나오는 대목에서, 하나님께서 모세에게 어떻게 말씀하셨는지를, 너희는 읽어보지 못하였느냐? 하나님께서는 모세에게 말씀하시기를 '나는 아브라함의 하나님이요, 이삭의 하나님이요, 야곱의 하나님이다' 하시지 않으셨느냐?
v.WLB: 죽은 사람의 부활에 대해서는 모세의 책에 가시떨기나무가 나오는 곳에서 하나님께서 모세에게 말씀하시기를 ‘나는 아브라함의 하나님, 이삭의 하나님, 야곱의 하나님이다’ 하신 것을 읽어 보지 못했느냐?

v.CNV: 他不是死人的　神，而是活人的　神。你们是大错特错了！”
v.ESV: He is not God of the dead, but of the living. You are quite wrong.”
v.GAE: 하나님은 죽은 자의 하나님이 아니요 산 자의 하나님이시라 너희가 크게 오해하였도다 하시니라
v.KJV: He is not the God of the dead, but the God of the living: ye therefore do greatly err.
v.NASB: 'He is not the God of the dead, but of the living; you are greatly mistaken.'
v.NIV: He is not the God of the dead, but of the living. You are badly mistaken!”
v.NRSV: He is God not of the dead, but of the living; you are quite wrong.”
v.SAENEW: 하나님은 죽은 사람들의 하나님이 아니라, 살아 있는 사람들의 하나님이시다. 너희는 생각을 크게 잘못 하고 있다."
v.WLB: 하나님께서는 죽은 사람들의 하나님이 아니라 살아 있는 사람들의 하나님이시다. 너희가 크게 잘못 생각하고 있다.”

v.CNV: 有一个经学家，听到他们的辩论，觉得耶稣回答得好，就来问他：“诫命中哪一条是第一重要的呢？”
v.ESV: And one of the scribes came up and heard them disputing with one another, and seeing that he answered them well, asked him, “Which commandment is the most important of all?”
v.GAE: 서기관 중 한 사람이 그들이 변론하는 것을 듣고 예수께서 잘 대답하신 줄을 알고 나아와 묻되 모든 계명 중에 첫째가 무엇이니이까
v.KJV: And one of the scribes came, and having heard them reasoning together, and perceiving that he had answered them well, asked him, Which is the first commandment of all?
v.NASB: One of the scribes came and heard them arguing, and recognizing that He had answered them well, asked Him, 'What commandment is the foremost of all?'
v.NIV: One of the teachers of the law came and heard them debating. Noticing that Jesus had given them a good answer, he asked him, “Of all the commandments, which is the most important?”
v.NRSV: One of the scribes came near and heard them disputing with one another, and seeing that he answered them well, he asked him, “Which commandment is the first of all?”
v.SAENEW: 율법학자들 가운데 한 사람이 다가와서, 그들이 변론하는 것을 들었다. 그는 예수가 그들에게 대답을 잘 하시는 것을 보고서, 예수께 물었다. "모든 계명 가운데서 가장 으뜸되는 것은 어느 것입니까?"
v.WLB: 율법학자들 가운데 한 사람이 와서 그들이 논쟁하는 것을 들었습니다. 예수께서 그들에게 대답 잘하시는 것을 보고 예수께 물었습니다. “모든 계명들 가운데 어떤 것이 가장 중요한 계명입니까?”

v.CNV: 耶稣回答：“第一重要的是：‘以色列啊，你要听！主我们的　神是独一的主。
v.ESV: Jesus answered, “The most important is, ‘Hear, O Israel: The Lord our God, the Lord is one.
v.GAE: 예수께서 대답하시되 첫째는 이것이니 이스라엘아 들으라 주 곧 우리 하나님은 유일한 주시라
v.KJV: And Jesus answered him, The first of all the commandments is, Hear, O Israel; The Lord our God is one Lord:
v.NASB: Jesus answered, 'The foremost is, 'HEAR, O ISRAEL! THE LORD OUR GOD IS ONE LORD;
v.NIV: “The most important one,” answered Jesus, “is this: ‘Hear, O Israel: The Lord our God, the Lord is one.
v.NRSV: Jesus answered, “The first is, ‘Hear, O Israel: the Lord our God, the Lord is one;
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 대답하셨다. "첫째는 이것이다. '이스라엘아, 들어라. 우리 하나님이신 주님은 오직 한 분이신 주님이시다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 대답하셨습니다. “첫째로 중요한 계명은 이것이다. ‘이스라엘아, 들으라! 주 우리 하나님은 오직 한 분이시다.

v.CNV: 你要全心、全性、全意、全力，爱主你的　神。’
